title: Unidentified dianoga-like species
content:

A [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopod]] '''species''' existed that had one [[Eye/Legends|eye]] mounted on a single [[Ommatophora|eyestalk]]<ref name="CCG" /> and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with [[Limb/Legends|appendages]] on both sides at the base of their eyestalk. The [[Species/Legends|species]] had anterior flipper-like appendages and a long, flexible tail.<ref name="CSWE" /> At least one member had [[Color/Legends|reddish-brown]] skin with black stripes and a hazel eye.<ref name="CCG" /> They were similar to the [[Dianoga/Legends|dianoga]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a species [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vodran/Legends|Vodran]].<ref name="Vodran CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|310|Vodran}}</ref> The species'<ref name="CSWE" /> culture controlled their environment through [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] abilities.<ref name="CCG" />

[[Niado Duegad]] was a [[Force-attunement|Force-attuned]] member of the species who hailed from the dianoga's homeworld. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Duegad worked as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], and his enhanced [[adrenal gland]]s allowed for short bursts of incredible strength.<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The species originated as one of several [[Alien/Legends|alien]] maquettes created for possible inclusion in the scenes taking place in [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|desert palace]] during the production stages of [[1983]]'s [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. Known as "Alien 18," the maquette was among the ones scrapped before the film's release as its non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] nature made it too difficult to realize as a puppet.<ref name="Chronicles">''[[Star Wars Chronicles]]''</ref> In [[1998]], the ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' expansion set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] made a card for Niado Duegad, showing an image of the maquette and thus bringing the species into the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="CCG" />
